content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
65	
66	## User Interaction
67	
68	Users provide hints in two places:
69	
70	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
71	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
72	
73	## Agent Roles
74	
75	### Plan Agent
76	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
77	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
78	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
79	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
80	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
81	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
82	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
83	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
84	
85	### Prover Agent
86	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
87	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
88	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
89	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
90	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
91	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
92	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
93	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
94	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
95	
96	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
97	
98	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
99	
100	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
101	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
102	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
103	
104	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
105	
106	### Review Agent
107	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
108	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
109	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
110	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
111	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
112	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	prover  (iteration 047 — Phase A step 6 *Path 2* / Serre-finiteness scaffolding **foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure**: append four declarations to `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` namespace `AlgebraicGeometry`, after iter-012's `cechCohomology_OC` (around L809), before `end AlgebraicGeometry` (L811): (1) `noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain k C F 𝒰` — parameterised Čech cochain complex over any sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`, generalising iter-012's specialisation to `F = toModuleKSheaf C`; (2) `noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n` — parameterised cohomology; (3) `theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` — `rfl`-bridge stating iter-012's specialisation; (4) `theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` — analogous for cohomology. Iter-046 closure verified (this pass): all eight iter-046 declarations land cleanly at L40–98 (Block A `namespace CategoryTheory`) + L711, L733, L762 (Block B inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`); sorry count `9 → 9`; file LOC `670 → 811` (+141, within plan estimate band of +120-160). Plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirms all four iter-047 declarations land cleanly `{success: true, diagnostics: []}` end-to-end with kernel-only axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. Iter-047 sorry trajectory `9 → 9` (no transient — bodies probe-confirmed). Iter-047 lays foundational Čech scaffolding that the substantive iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem will build on. The iter-012 specialisations remain unchanged in scope; downstream consumers are not broken. Scope deliberately narrowed from "produce IsAffineHModuleVanishing carrier" (multi-iteration substantive; not feasible in one pass) to "lay foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure" — see STRATEGY.md and task_pending.md for the iter-048+ decomposition (comparison map → IsAcyclicCover class → producer instances). Phase A iterations-remaining estimate revised UP from ~3 to ~6 (STRATEGY.md Estimations table) reflecting the substantive depth of the remaining Čech-vs-derived comparison + affine Čech vanishing work.
5	
6	## Stages
7	- [x] init
8	- [x] autoformalize
9	- [ ] prover
10	- [ ] polish
11	
12	## Big-picture context
13	
14	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
15	
16	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (Serre finiteness on a proper curve via the Mayer-Vietoris LES + affine Čech vanishing) has been the iter-009 → iter-046 push, building the `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology infrastructure end-to-end through (a) the iter-009/010/014/015 H⁰ algebraic bridges, (b) the iter-016 → iter-026 verbatim Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` mirror (split iter-027 into a dedicated file), (c) the iter-028 → iter-037 cover-totality bridge (Steps 3 + 3.5 + finrank corollary + Module.Finite transport companions), (d) the iter-038/039 H⁰ Module.Finite transports, (e) the iter-040/041 (mis-designed)/042/043 affine + wholespace H⁰/H>0 carrier predicates, (f) the iter-044 geometric Stein input, (g) the iter-045 global-sections evaluation iso, and (h) the iter-046 producer chain end-to-end for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` (8 declarations across two blocks; closes the H⁰ Module.Finite ladder via iter-043's curve consumer firing automatically). **Iter-047 (this iteration)** opens the second-half push: foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure (4 declarations, ~30-50 LOC), generalising iter-012's structure-sheaf-specific Čech carriers to any sheaf F, laying scaffolding for the iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem (the substantive multi-iteration step that delivers `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`).
17	
18	## Iteration 047 plan-agent summary (this iteration)
19	
20	**Iter-046 verification.** Plan-agent verification (this pass) confirms iter-046 closure landed cleanly:
21	
22	- **Sorry count**: `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary` returns `9 total across 3 file(s)` (5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`). Pre-iter-046 `9`, post-iter-046 `9`, target post-iter-047 prover `9`.
23	- **File diagnostics**: `lean_diagnostic_messages` on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`. Zero warnings, zero errors.
24	- **File LOC**: `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` 811 LOC (iter-046 added 141 LOC = 670 → 811; +141 vs +120-160 plan estimate band — within band).
25	- **Iter-046 declarations placed correctly**: Block A (CategoryTheory gap-fills) at L40–98; Block B (Scheme applied + producer) at L711, L733, L762.
26	- **`Genus.lean` preserved**: still 0 sorries; iter-011 first-protected closure intact.
27	- **`archon-protected.yaml`**: `git diff archon-protected.yaml` returns empty. Unchanged.
28	- **No new axioms**: no new `axiom` declarations in `AlgebraicJacobian/`.
29	- **Iter-046 axiom check**:
30	  - `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf` → `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}` — kernel-only.
31	- **Cosmetic prover deviation acknowledged**: the plan's `include adj in lemma ...` per-declaration syntax was rejected by the local Lean toolchain ("unexpected token 'include'; expected 'lemma'"). Prover switched to section-style `include adj` on its own line, mirroring Mathlib's pattern in `CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Additive.lean` L36. No semantic change. Recorded in iter-046 task_result and migrated to task_done.
32	
33	**Iter-047 work scope.** With iter-046's producer chain closing the H⁰ Module.Finite ladder, the next semantic step in Phase A step 6 is the parallel H>0 affine vanishing producer `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. **This is multi-iteration substantive work** requiring (i) Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem (Mathlib gap; project-local construction), (ii) affine Čech vanishing for the structure sheaf (computational Koszul-like exactness on Spec A); each is itself non-trivial. **Iter-047 is therefore narrowed in scope to a single foundational sub-step** that lays groundwork without trying to close the full producer chain in one pass.
34	
35	**Mathlib re-probe (this pass).**
36	
37	1. `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean` has `cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` — the same Čech-side carrier already used by iter-012 to define `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`.
38	2. **No Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem exists in Mathlib.** No `cechCohomology_iso_derivedFunctor`-style lemma. No "acyclic cover" predicate. The iter-048+ work must build this.
39	3. **No scheme-level Serre vanishing in Mathlib.** No `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Cohomology/` directory; only `EllAdicCohomology` (étale-adic) and abstract `RightDerived` machinery. The `subsingleton_H_of_isZero` lemma is trivial (vanishes when the sheaf itself is zero).
40	4. **Informal agent unavailable**: Gemini API key reported leaked, returns 403 PERMISSION_DENIED. Plan-agent scoped iter-047 from own analysis without external help.
41	
42	**Iter-047 work scope (final).** Append four foundational parameterised Čech declarations to `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` inside the existing `namespace AlgebraicGeometry` block (after iter-012's `cechCohomology_OC`, around L809, before `end AlgebraicGeometry`):
43	
44	1. **`Scheme.cechCochain`** — parameterised Čech cochain complex over any sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Body: `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)`.
45	2. **`Scheme.cechCohomology`** — parameterised cohomology. Body: `(Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n`.
46	3. **`Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq`** — `rfl`-bridge stating `cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰`.
47	4. **`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq`** — analogous for cohomology.
48	
49	All four bodies probe-confirmed via `lean_run_code` (this pass) with kernel-only axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. The `_eq` lemmas are `rfl` because iter-012's specialisations are *definitionally* the `F = toModuleKSheaf C` instance of the iter-047 parameterised forms. The iter-012 declarations remain unchanged in scope — downstream consumers (none yet, but anticipated for iter-048+) keep working.
50	
51	**Iter-048+ next** (informational only, not assigned this iteration): see STRATEGY.md and task_pending.md for the decomposition. Briefly: iter-048 candidate = comparison map `cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`; iter-049 candidate = `class IsAcyclicCover` predicate + immediate consumer; iter-050+ = producer instances for basic-open covers of affine schemes (substantive Koszul-like computation); iter-051 = the `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` producer chaining everything; iter-052 = `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` (the genus carrier; closes Phase A step 6).
52	
53	## Current Objectives
54	
55	### 1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`** — append four foundational parameterised Čech declarations
56	
57	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex` § *Foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure (iter-047)* (newly written this pass) — contains `def:Scheme_cechCochain`, `def:Scheme_cechCohomology`, `thm:Scheme_cechCochain_OC_eq`, `thm:Scheme_cechCohomology_OC_eq`.
58	
59	**Append four declarations inside the existing `namespace AlgebraicGeometry` block, after iter-012's `cechCohomology_OC` (currently ending around L809), before the existing `end AlgebraicGeometry` (around L811).** No new imports required — all needed names (`cechComplexFunctor`, `sheafToPresheaf`, `Sheaf`, `ModuleCat`, `Opens.grothendieckTopology`, `CochainComplex`, `HomologicalComplex.homology`) are transitively in scope (verified by plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe; same scope as iter-012).
60	
61	#### Probe-confirmed bodies (this pass; copy verbatim)
62	
63	```lean
64	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
65	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
66	sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
67	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
68	underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
69	`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`. Iter-012's specialisation
70	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
71	(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).
72	
73	This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived
74	comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
75	`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`
76	(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
77	the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
78	generality. -/
79	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
80	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
81	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
82	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
83	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
84	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)
85	
86	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cohomology generalising iter-012's
87	`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised Čech
88	cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
89	specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
90	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
91	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
92	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
93	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
94	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
95	  (Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n
96	
97	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
98	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
99	proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj
100	((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
101	consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
102	form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
103	theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
104	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
105	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
106	    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 :=
107	  rfl
108	
109	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
110	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
111	theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
112	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
113	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
114	    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n =
115	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n :=
116	  rfl
117	```
118	
119	#### Notes for the prover
120	
121	- **Placement**: append all four declarations inside the existing `namespace AlgebraicGeometry` block at the file end. They go between iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` (currently ending around L809) and the closing `end AlgebraicGeometry` (around L811). Use the `Scheme.` qualified prefix on the declared names (matches iter-012 — the `namespace AlgebraicGeometry` is open, but `Scheme` is *not* open inside it, so the `Scheme.` prefix is required to land inside `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`).
122	- **No new imports**: probe-confirmed unnecessary. All names (`cechComplexFunctor`, `sheafToPresheaf`, `Opens.grothendieckTopology`, `Sheaf`, `ModuleCat.{u}`, `CochainComplex`, `HomologicalComplex.homology`) are in scope via the existing `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb` chain.
123	- **`noncomputable` is required** on declarations 1 + 2 (they invoke noncomputable Mathlib infrastructure: `cechComplexFunctor`, `homology`).
124	- **`theorem` (not `instance`)** on declarations 3 + 4: they are propositional equalities, not typeclass producers.
125	- **Body shape rigid**: probe-confirmed exactly as written. 1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)

770	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
771	    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
772	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
773	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
774	    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
775	
776	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
777	
778	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
779	
780	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the Čech cochain complex of
781	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
782	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
783	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
784	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
785	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
786	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`.
787	
788	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
789	downstream comparison theorem (Čech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
790	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
791	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the Čech-side carrier. -/
792	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
793	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
794	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
795	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
796	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
797	
798	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th Čech cohomology
799	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
800	`n`-th homology of the Čech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
801	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
802	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
803	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
804	acyclic. -/
805	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
806	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
807	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
808	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
809	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n
810	
811	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: sheafification, Ext, and the structure sheaf
10	
11	Phase A step 5 (per `STRATEGY.md`):
12	
13	* **Prerequisites** (iter-005, closed): the `HasSheafify` and `HasExt` instances
14	  on the topology of opens of an arbitrary topological space, valued in
15	  `ModuleCat k`. The `Linear k` enrichment on the resulting sheaf category is
16	  auto-inferable from Mathlib and therefore needs no scaffold here; together
17	  with `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule` it gives the path to a
18	  `Module k` structure on `Ext` groups.
19	* **Main** (iter-006, scaffolded here): the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme
20	  `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` viewed as a sheaf of `k`-modules. Built
21	  from the structure-morphism-derived ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` (helpers (1)–(5))
22	  and packaged as the presheaf `toModuleKPresheaf` (6), proven to be a sheaf
23	  by `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (7), and bundled as `toModuleKSheaf` (8).
24	
25	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 006 — refactor scaffold)
28	
29	The two prerequisite declarations (lines below) are honestly closed (iter-005).
30	The eight Phase A step 5 main declarations are scaffolded as `sorry`. The

  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n

/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`. Iter-012's specialisation
`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).

This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived
comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`
(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
generality. -/
noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)

/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cohomology generalising iter-012's
`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised Čech
cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
  (Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n

/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj
((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 :=
  rfl

/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n =
      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n :=
  rfl

end AlgebraicGeometry

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean

## Iter-047 — foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure

### Result: RESOLVED

Appended four new declarations to `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`, between iter-012's
`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` (now ending at L809) and the closing `end AlgebraicGeometry`
(now at L871). Used the verbatim probe-confirmed bodies from `PROGRESS.md`.

### Declarations added (placement: AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean)

1. **`Scheme.cechCochain`** — `noncomputable def`, parameterised Čech cochain
   complex over any sheaf `F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology …) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
   Body: `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)`. (≈ L811–L832)
2. **`Scheme.cechCohomology`** — `noncomputable def`, the `n`-th homology of
   `Scheme.cechCochain`. (≈ L834–L843)
3. **`Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq`** — `theorem` (`rfl`-bridge) stating that
   iter-012's `cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰`. (≈ L845–L856)
4. **`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq`** — `theorem` (`rfl`-bridge) stating the
   analogous equality for cohomology. (≈ L858–L866)

### Verification (post-edit)

- `lean_diagnostic_messages` on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`
  → `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` — no errors, no warnings.
- `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cechCochain` → axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no warnings.
- `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cechCohomology` → axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no warnings.
- `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` → axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no warnings.
- `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` → axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no warnings.

All four declarations land kernel-only.

### Sorry trajectory

`9 → 9` (no new sorries introduced; iter-012's specialisations preserved unchanged;
no other declarations touched).

### File LOC

Pre-iter-047: 811. Post-iter-047: 871 (+60). Within plan estimate band.

### Notes / Deviations

- No deviations from the plan. Verbatim probe-confirmed bodies used, including
  argument order, namespace prefix `Scheme.`, and `noncomputable`/`theorem`
  modifiers.
- No new imports required (transitively in scope via existing
  `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb`).
- Iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` and `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` left
  untouched; the `rfl` bridges rely on definitional equality between the
  iter-012 specialisation and the iter-047 parameterised form at
  `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.

### Blueprint markers ready

- `def:Scheme_cechCohomology` — `\leanok`.
- `thm:Scheme_cechCochain_OC_eq` — `\leanok` (proof closed by `rfl`).
- `thm:Scheme_cechCohomology_OC_eq` — `\leanok` (proof closed by `rfl`).

(Marker writes are the review agent's responsibility.)

### Next steps (informational; out of iter-047 scope)

Iter-048+ substantive work: comparison map
`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`,
